name: Campo Real, Tielmes, Morata de Tajuña, Arganda del Rey – circular route
name_original: Campo Real, Tielmes, Morata de Tajuña, Arganda del Rey – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Campo Real, located in the province of Madrid, Spain, traverse a diverse landscape. The region is characterized by the Jarama riverbed, featuring cliffs and varied vegetation, including moorlands and traditional livestock routes known as "Vías Pecuarias." Cyclists can expect a mix of terrains, from relatively flat sections along the Tajuña Greenway to more challenging climbs through scrubland and holm oak groves. This area provides excellent road cycling Campo Real routes for exploring the natural environment. Best road cycling routes in Campo Real The most popular road cycling route is Arganda del Rey, Campo Real, Vía Verde del Tajuña – circular route, a 46.3 miles (74.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This circular route incorporates sections of the Tajuña Greenway. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Campo Real, Tielmes, Morata de Tajuña, Arganda del Rey – circular route, a moderate 46.7 miles (75.2 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through several towns, including Tielmes and Morata de Tajuña. Local road cyclists also love the Tajuña Greenway – Plaza del Pueblo de Loeches loop from Arganda del Rey, a 42.8 miles (68.9 km) trail leading through the Tajuña Greenway, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Campo Real is defined by the Jarama riverbed, cliffs, and the Tajuña Greenway. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult climbs. The routes in Campo Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 3,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Campo Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Campo Real?
A: There are over 130 road cycling routes around Campo Real. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from easier rides to more challenging climbs, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Campo Real?
A: The terrain around Campo Real is quite varied. You'll find a mix of relatively flat sections, particularly along the Jarama riverbed, and more challenging climbs. The region features cliffs and diverse vegetation, offering scenic backdrops for your rides.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Campo Real?
A: Yes, Campo Real offers several easier road cycling routes. Out of the over 130 routes, 10 are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Campo Real often pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Poveda Railway Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a valuable natural reserve. The region's landscape also includes the Jarama riverbed, cliffs, and diverse vegetation.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Campo Real?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Campo Real are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977041797/arganda-del-rey-campo-real-via-verde-del-tajuna-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arganda del Rey, Campo Real, Vía Verde del Tajuña – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that incorporates sections of the Tajuña Greenway.

Q: What is the Tajuña Greenway and how can I access it for road cycling?
A: The Tajuña Greenway (Vía Verde del Tajuña) is a charming bike path built on an old railway line, offering a largely traffic-free and picturesque route. It starts near La Poveda, close to Campo Real, and extends for approximately 60 kilometers. You can access sections of it through routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway – Tajuña Greenway loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Campo Real?
A: The road cycling routes in Campo Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. Over 3,700 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Campo Real offers 41 difficult routes. These often include more significant elevation gains and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa Climb – Plaza Mayor de Chinchón loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial ascents.

Q: Can I find routes that connect Campo Real with other nearby towns?
A: Yes, many routes connect Campo Real with neighboring towns, offering varying distances and ascents. You can cycle to places like Pozuelo del Rey, Tielmes, Morata de Tajuña, and Arganda del Rey. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977087582/campo-real-tielmes-morata-de-tajuna-arganda-del-rey-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Real, Tielmes, Morata de Tajuña, Arganda del Rey – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 75 km.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Campo Real?
A: The region's climate generally allows for cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred, while winter rides are possible but may require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any historical sites to visit along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Besides the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Poveda Railway Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, you might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3300745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornicabra Station and Quarries&lt;/a&gt;, which add a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there any quiet road cycling paths in the Campo Real area?
A: Many routes in the Campo Real area utilize quiet roads and dedicated paths, particularly sections of the Tajuña Greenway, which is built on an old railway line. This provides a more serene cycling experience away from heavy traffic.
tips: The Hermitage of the Virgen de Oliva de Valdilecha was built in the 17th century. It is located in a place that offers a magnificent view of the valley.
It is part of the old architectural complex of the city fortress and dates from the Middle Ages.
Nice stretch of tarmac, starts (from Morata) with a nice short climb and then continues with a series of rolling hills in between olive trees and spectacular views.

Arganda has relevant historical monuments, for example the Church of San Juan Bautista, built at the end of the 17th century, with its characteristic 57-meter tower. The Casa del Rey also stands out, a small palace from the end of the 16th century.
